topics.title: Basic Sentence Structure
topics.explanation:
Romanian follows Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) order like English.

Romanian has definite and indefinite articles. The definite article is attached to the END of the noun (enclitic):
• carte (book) → cartea (the book)
• om (man) → omul (the man)

quiz.question: Where is the definite article placed in Romanian?
options: Attached to the END of the noun

quiz.question: What is the basic word order in Romanian?

topics.title: Noun Gender
topics.explanation:
Romanian has three genders: masculine, feminine, and neuter.

• Masculine: ends in consonant (om, băiat)
• Feminine: ends in -ă or -e (femeie, casă)
• Neuter: masculine in singular, feminine in plural (scaun/scaune)

examples.target: un om bun · o femeie bună · un scaun bun
examples.transliteration: oon om boon · o feh-mey-eh boo-nuh · oon ska-oon boon
examples.english: a good man (m) · a good woman (f) · a good chair (n)

quiz.question: Which ending is typical for feminine nouns?
